ref: Meanwhile, Jacob heard what had happened. But his sons were out in the fields with the cattle, so he did not do anything at the time.
text: Eye Yakob se be, wogblẽ vianyɔnu Dina, ke viaŋutsuwo le gbedzi le eƒe alẽhawo gbɔ, eye Yakob zi kpi, vaseɖe esime wogbɔ.
translated: And Jacob said, when he saw the sons of Dinah, his daughter, and his men and all his livestock, then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Woaɖe miaƒe aʋanuyiwo ɖa, eye esia anye mia kple mia dome nubabla la ƒe dzesi.
translated: When they were going, they saw a great crowd with their Lord and his disciples.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.
translated: The Lord is good to all, and his mercy is over all that he has made, the earth is full of his riches, and also the sea.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.
translated: When I came to the crossroads, I asked the elders, they said, it is a taboo, the taboo of our ancestors and the laws of our ancestors' taboos.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko! Mawu ɖo Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.
translated: And Abraham said to God, "Oh, that Ishmael might live before you!" God said, "No, but Sarah your wife shall bear you a son."

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.
translated: And Leah said, now my husband will love me, for I have borne him three sons, and she named him Zebulon.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
